predictions.title: GGUF supply chains are the new battleground.
predictions.body: **GGUF supply chains are the new battleground.** SGLang CVE-2026-5760 (Jinja2 SSTI through GGUF, RCE) makes visible the fact that "model files themselves are carriers of executable code." By Q3 2026, Hugging Face / Ollama Library / ModelScope will require model-card + chat_template signature verification or sandboxed execution as a baseline; Foundry Local / Bedrock / vLLM will enable SSTI scanning by default at GGUF load time. In plain language: In plain language: In plain language:

reasoning.because: SGLang CVE-2026-5760 ships a Jinja2 SSTI in GGUF chat_template parsing yielding RCE on model load.
reasoning.given: GGUF files embed executable templates, so model artifacts function as carriers of arbitrary code.
reasoning.so_that: Hubs gate uploads behind signed model-cards and chat_templates, and serving runtimes scan for SSTI at GGUF load time.
reasoning.landing: By Q3 2026; HF / Ollama / ModelScope + Foundry Local / Bedrock / vLLM.
reasoning.plain_language: A bug in SGLang shows model files can hide harmful code, so big model hubs and inference servers will start checking them before running.

predictions.summary: SGLang CVE-2026-5760, a Jinja2 SSTI through GGUF chat_template parsing, shows that model files carry executable code. By Q3 2026, Hugging Face, Ollama, and ModelScope are expected to require signed model-cards or sandboxes, and Foundry Local, Bedrock, and vLLM to enable SSTI scanning at GGUF load.

predictions.title: The era of "exclusive alliance between hyperscaler and frontier lab
predictions.body: **The era of "exclusive alliance between hyperscaler and frontier lab"** is here. Amazon-Anthropic's 10-year $100B commit becomes the second "hardware-codesigned + platform-exclusive" contract after MS-OpenAI. Through H2 2026 / H1 2027, expect the **5-pole regime** — Google–(in-house Gemini), Anthropic–AWS, OpenAI–MS, Meta–internal (Llama), xAI–Oracle/NVDA — to lock in. Trainium / TPU / MAIA / MI300X design differences will translate directly into model-behavior differences, and developers will reassess **backend portability** (a runtime-agnostic layer like MCP) as a hard requirement. In plain language: In plain language: In plain language:

reasoning.because: Amazon-Anthropic 10-year $100B + 5 GW Trainium pairs with MS-OpenAI as 2nd codesigned exclusive
reasoning.given: Frontier training economics demand one hyperscaler with co-designed silicon to fund cluster build
reasoning.so_that: A 5-pole lab-cloud-chip regime locks in and runtime-agnostic backend portability becomes a hard requirement
reasoning.landing: H2 2026 / H1 2027; 5-pole regime locked + portability layer adopted
reasoning.plain_language: Each big AI lab is pairing with one cloud and one chip family, so apps will need a translator layer to switch between them.

predictions.summary: Amazon-Anthropic's 10-year $100B Trainium commit joins MS-OpenAI as the 2nd hardware-codesigned hyperscaler-lab exclusive. Through H2 2026 / H1 2027 a 5-pole regime locks in across Google, Anthropic-AWS, OpenAI-MS, Meta, and xAI, forcing backend portability as a hard requirement.

predictions.title: Proprietary re-expansion against an open-weight geopolitical split.
predictions.body: **Proprietary re-expansion against an open-weight geopolitical split.** Qwen3.6-Max-Preview's "proprietary, API-only" pivot, alongside Anthropic's staged gating of Mythos Preview, makes Q3 2026 the moment **"strongest model = closed weights"** crystallizes. Open weights, meanwhile, concentrate on the 35B-A3B / Bonsai / Llama lines for production, formalizing the line: local for utility, cloud for the frontier. In plain language: In plain language: In plain language:

reasoning.because: Qwen3.6-Max-Preview pivoted API-only and Anthropic gated Mythos Preview in the same window
reasoning.given: frontier-tier compute + safety overhead make open-weight release uneconomic for top-of-stack labs
reasoning.so_that: by Q3 2026 the strongest publicly available models are uniformly closed, with open weights settling at the 35B-A3B / Bonsai / Llama production tier
reasoning.landing: by Q3 2026; ≥2 frontier labs API-only + open-weight ceiling at ~35B-A3B class
reasoning.plain_language: The biggest models are going behind paywalls while smaller open ones stay free, so people will use local models for everyday tasks and cloud for the hardest ones.

predictions.summary: Qwen3.6-Max-Preview's API-only pivot and Anthropic's gating of Mythos Preview together mark Q3 2026 as the point where the strongest models are uniformly closed weights. Open-weight releases concentrate on the 35B-A3B, Bonsai, and Llama production tiers, formalizing a split between local utility models and cloud frontier models.
